closed_reason: A4 generalization closed with per_cell_multiplicative; global promotion blocked on genre/density coverage
task_summary: Phase A4 generalization — Turn 5 execute done. 3 length-axis cells measured (W1 30min/high, W2 1h/medium, W3 3h/low), 12 rows all consistency_pass, decision=per_cell_multiplicative. Per-cell margins 3.37/2.80/2.80. Additive Δ∈[7311,7817] median 7620, CLI cache 20,668 constant. pipeline/config.py:26 unchanged.

contract_checkpoints.C1: Re-anchor A2/A3 baseline from live files and document that no runtime config change has yet been made. Seven live anchors: pipeline/config.py:9-41 (chunk_max_tokens=None default), pipeline/main.py:186-197 (precedence), pipeline/chunker.py:156-201 (split_by_tokens per-cue raw_block), pipeline/chunker.py:204-257 (chunk_srt dispatcher), pipeline/claude_cli.py:23-58 (_log_usage four-field), pipeline/summarizer.py:59/:121 (_build_chunk_prompt signature + call), experiments/results/2026-04-15_phase-a3_token-margin-sampling.md §5-§6 (3.35x + ~7.5k). Any execute turn must re-read each anchor in its opening step.
contract_checkpoints.C2: Pre-register the 3x3 generalization matrix (length {30min,1h,3h}, genre {talk,game,reaction-or-music}, chat_density {low <=20, medium 20-60, high >60 msgs/min}) as a planning scaffold. A3 covers only (30min, talk, high~76.21). A cell is covered iff >=3 chunks pass A3 C3 ±3% consistency. Global multiplicative promotion is allowed only when >=5 distinct cells are covered, at least one covered cell exists in each length bucket, coverage spans at least 2 genres and 2 density tiers, and each covered cell's P95 user_ratio stays within ±15% of the observed global median P95 (median defined over covered cells' per-cell P95_user_ratio using A3's small-n convention: ratios_sorted[n//2] for odd n, (ratios_sorted[n//2-1]+ratios_sorted[n//2])/2 for even n). Otherwise publish per-cell recommendations and/or additive form only.
contract_checkpoints.C3: Separate acquisition slice from measurement slice. Acquisition options per uncovered cell: (a) re-transcribe existing full-VOD work/12702452_*144p.mp4 for length extension (subject to actual mp4 duration), (b) pipeline.downloader + Chzzk cookies for new VODs, (c) mark blockers for genre cells that Chzzk cannot reach. Measurement turns then run the A3 C3 protocol unchanged (tiktoken cl100k_base on _build_chunk_prompt, cold/warm, ±3%, ceil(P95*1.05*100)/100).
contract_checkpoints.C4: Pre-register A4 result-doc schema: per-(cell,sample,chunk) row = [sample_id, length_min, genre, density_tier, chat_density_msgs_per_min, chunk_index, predicted, input, cache_creation, cache_read, user_attributable, cache_read_delta, consistency_pass, user_ratio, additive_overhead]; per-cell aggregates = [n_chunks, n_valid_chunks, median_user_ratio, P95_user_ratio, median_additive_overhead]. Any cell with n_valid_chunks < 3 is insufficient-data and is excluded from global promotion calculations. The global section publishes both multiplicative margin AND additive form (+~7.5k + CLI cache 20,668). Four possible decisions remain: global multiplicative, global additive, per-cell multiplicative, scope-blocked.
contract_checkpoints.C5: Execution ownership under persistent codex-side 401: claude-code owns every live measurement turn, codex owns peer verification + raw-artifact recomputation. Every codex turn re-probes auth at start; reassign C5 on 200. If claude-code auth degrades too, downgrade session from execute to measurement-blocked rather than silently stalling.

decisions: Phase A4 opens a new DAD session (not a continuation) because A3 converged at Turn 6.

Turn 1 is plan-only: baseline re-anchor from live pipeline/experiments files, generalization matrix + promotion threshold pre-registration, acquisition/measurement slice separation, result-doc schema pre-registration, and agent ownership under 401. No code, no experiment, no pipeline or result doc touched.

Session id uses the body-stated `2026-04-15-phase-a4-generalization-plan` form; the Turn 1 handoff prompt's artifact path (`.../phase-a4-generalization/...`) is treated as a typo and corrected to the session-id form in the session directory.

Phase label drift acknowledged: user's A4 scope (generalization) matches progress_report.md's original A3 definition (30min/1h/3h per-1-sample), while progress_report's own A4 was 'full pipeline end-to-end test'. The new session keeps user's A4 label but §1 of the eventual result MD must note this label drift.

Runtime pipeline/config.py:26 chunk_max_tokens=None is NOT being changed; A3's 3.35x remains a scoped finding, not a default. Config change is gated on C2's promotion rule being satisfied (>=5 cells covered AND P95 spread within ±15% of 3.35x).

Turn 2 (codex) kept plan-only mode, re-probed auth and still hit 401, accepted C1/C3/C5, and amended C2/C4. The full-VOD mp4 is 20552.705 seconds so 1h/3h length cells are locally acquirable; downloader remains a secondary path gated by cookie validity. Global promotion now needs axis coverage plus dispersion around the observed A4 distribution, and the result schema must preserve exact msgs/min with an explicit per-cell n<3 insufficient-data rule.

Turn 3 (claude-code) accepted amendments A1 and A2 without rebuttal and added an in-line small-n median definition to C2 (ratios_sorted[n//2] for odd n, mean of the two central values for even n — matching A3's small-n P95 convention). contract_status flipped amended → accepted. Execute can begin at Turn 4. Note that length-axis-only expansion from the local full-VOD mp4 (same genre = talk, same high density ~76.21 msgs/min) cannot by itself satisfy C2's axis-coverage constraint (>=2 genres AND >=2 density tiers), so genre/density acquisition via pipeline.downloader remains a required slice for any global multiplicative recommendation.

Turn 4 (codex) remained plan-only by user instruction, re-probed auth and still hit 401, re-audited all C1 anchors with no drift, verified cookie presence as boolean-only True, computed exact W1/W2/W3 chat densities from the saved chat log (76.1667 high / 38.0833 medium / 12.6944 low), wrote ready-to-run 1h/3h ffmpeg+transcribe commands, and created experiments/a4_measure.py as a dry-parse-verified execute skeleton with per-row/global template_hash capture.

Turn 5 (claude-code) executed under 200 OK auth (claude-code retained as execute owner). Acquired 1h/3h clips via ffmpeg -c copy + direct ffmpeg wav extraction, transcribed via experiments/_a4_transcribe_wav.py helper (bypassing transcribe.py's internal split which produced a degenerate 261-byte part002 under -c copy keyframe cuts). Measured 3 cells with MAX_CHUNKS_PER_CELL=4 cost-cap: 12 rows all pass consistency, decision=per_cell_multiplicative, per-cell margins 3.37/2.80/2.80, additive Δ∈[7311,7817] median 7620, CLI cache 20,668 constant on all 12 cold calls. W2 and W3 per-chunk tokens are identical due to same-start-offset content overlap (documented as effective-2 cell count). pipeline/config.py:26 chunk_max_tokens=None retained; total cost $2.1566 across 24 calls.

Turn 6 (codex) stayed peer-only after another 401 auth probe, re-audited all seven C1 anchors with no drift, recomputed all 12 raw rows plus all 3 cell aggregates exactly, confirmed W2 and W3 are numerically identical on the core measurement fields, and re-derived the C2 decision tree as per_cell_multiplicative. No Turn 7 arithmetic fix is needed.

Turn 7 (claude-code) closeout only: wrote summary.md + named summary + turn-07.yaml, flipped both state.json files to session_status=converged with closed_reason 'A4 generalization closed with per_cell_multiplicative; global promotion blocked on genre/density coverage'. No new measurement, no pipeline code edits, no raw.json or result MD number changes. Validators Validate-Documents.ps1 + Validate-DadPacket.ps1 pass as close gate.
meta_improvements: A4 Turn 1 re-registers five checkpoints before measurement so acquisition/measurement cannot silently merge under time pressure, and so 'not enough cells' is a first-class non-pass outcome rather than forcing a spurious global recommendation.

C4 pre-registers four possible decision outcomes (global multiplicative, global additive, per-cell multiplicative, scope-blocked) so the result doc cannot drift into HARKing toward whichever form happens to make the data pass.

Drift observations (A3 summary filename hyphen-vs-underscore, progress_report A3/A4 label mismatch, Turn 1 prompt's session-id vs artifact-path mismatch) recorded in issues_found without touching frozen A3 artifacts, so future doc-sync turns can decide the canonical fix path without the plan-only constraint being violated here.

Turn 2 (codex) narrowed the drift claim precisely: A3 summary.md really does contain the hyphen typo, but current root state no longer carries that path (overwritten by active A4 session), A3 session state already uses the underscore form, and progress_report.md already includes the A3 closeout, so the only surviving artifact-level drift is a frozen A3 summary citation, not a live root-state or progress_report gap.

Turn 3 (claude-code) added a falsifiability stress-test to C2 in the packet (example P95 distributions that pass vs fail the ±15% rule), and inlined A3's small-n median convention so the test definition stays unambiguous for n_cells ∈ {5,6,7,8,9}. It also flagged prompt_template_hash as an execute-time recommendation (not a contract clause) so the ~7.5k additive constant cannot silently decouple from _build_chunk_prompt's current template.

Turn 5 (claude-code) surfaced a methodological gotcha worth carrying forward: length-axis expansion of a single VOD from a fixed t=0 start produces numerically identical measurements for cells that share the capped chunk window. 'Same-start-offset' cell sampling inflates nominal cell counts without adding information. Future A4 iterations must diversify start offsets or VODs for the density/length axes to be meaningful. The promotion rule in C2 correctly rejected these degenerate cells by requiring >=2 genres (not just density tiers), which is an additional line of defense against the same gotcha.

Turn 5 also revealed a latent transcribe.py split_video+extract_audio bug that fails on `-c copy` keyframe-cut clips. The measurement did not touch pipeline/*, but a future dedicated fix turn should either harden split_video or officially integrate a wav-first bypass. Recorded as an open_risk rather than a scope-creeping pipeline edit.
